title: BYD’s luxury SUV received a record 150,000 orders, and now it’s headed overseas
summary: The Great Tang is the largest, most luxurious SUV to launch under the BYD brand. After a hot start in China, racking up a record 150,000 orders ahead of launch, BYD is bringing the flagship electric SUV overseas, including Europe, by the end of 2026.

title: Podcast: we drive Aptera’s solar car, Tesla Cybercab specs revealed, Lucid Cosmos design leaks, and more
summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Jamie’s first drive in Aptera’s solar car, Tesla Cybercab specs revealed, Lucid Cosmos design leak, and more.

title: One giant US power line, enough wind power for 1 million homes
summary: SunZia, an $11 billion wind-and-power-line project and one of the largest US clean energy transmission projects ever built, is officially online.

title: Hyundai is giving the Kona EV a complete overhaul, and it already looks like an upgrade
summary: The Kona EV is making a comeback, but it will look much different this time with Hyundai’s new design and latest infotainment setup.

title: 400 hp Mercedes-Benz VLE 300 combines limo luxury with lots of legroom
summary: The newest Mercedes-Benz VLE 300 electric MPV combines 800V battery tech with limousine-level luxury, top-shelf infotainment options, room for eight, and enough rear legroom to make an S-Class feel like a mini Cooper.

title: Aventon Aventure smart e-bikes at new lows, Yozma IN 10 dirt bike exclusive new $879 low, Jackery power stations, Segway, more
summary:
We’re closing out this week’s Green Deals with a bang! Leading the charge of Early Prime deals is Aventon’s newly launched 4th of July Sale with new and returning smart e-bike low prices, like the Aventure 3 All-Terrain e-bikes at a new $1,749 low + the Aventure M Mid-Drive All-Terrain e-bike at a new $2,649 low, among others. We also secured exclusive $320 savings on the Yozma IN 10 Electric Dirt Bike starting from a new $879 low, as well as the best current pricing on Jackery’s 1,070Wh Explorer 1000 V2, 2,042Wh Explorer 2000 V2, and 5,040Wh Explorer 5000 Plus power stations starting from $429. We also spotted Segway’s 40-mile Max G30P Electric Scooter back at its lowest price, as well as a new Early Prime low price on the Govee Matter Outdoor Lamp Post. All this and more are waiting for you below, and don’t forget about all the hangover deals either, which have been collected together at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s up to 60% Father’s Day and Prime Day savings from Heybike and Hiboy, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: BMW can’t wait: i3 orders open months early as buyers rush in
summary: The new i3 is arriving sooner than expected. Although it wasn’t expected to go on sale until this fall, BMW has already opened orders in several markets after “high levels” of early interest.

title: Jeep’s off-road EV goes on sale for $67,000, but its range is a bit disappointing
summary: Jeep’s first real off-road EV is finally here. The 2026 Jeep Recon EV is now on sale, starting at $67,000, but its driving range comes up a bit short of expectations.

title: Telo confirms 400 kW sustained charging for its tiny electric truck
summary:
Telo confirmed that its Mini-sized MT1 electric truck will charge at 400 kW sustained — a charging rate that puts it in the company of luxury EVs costing two to three times as much.

The startup also revealed a dual 800V/400V split-pack battery architecture designed to maximize performance on any fast charger, not just the latest high-voltage stations.

title: Maserati boosts Folgore EV range by up to 85 km in 2026 refresh
summary:
Maserati revealed updates to its Folgore electric lineup, with the GranTurismo Folgore now claiming over 540 km (335 miles) of WLTP range — an improvement of roughly 85 km over the previous version thanks to new energy management algorithms.

The range gains come as Maserati fights for relevance amid a sales collapse that saw deliveries drop to just 7,900 units in 2025, down 30% year-over-year and the lowest since 2012.

title: Skipping The Ferrari Luce Could Cost Collectors The Cars They Really Want
summary: Ferrari’s first EV may be controversial, but for loyal collectors, saying no could make the next dream allocation more difficult.

title: The 2027 Porsche Taycan Gets Fake Gear Shifts And A Bigger Standard Battery
summary: The refreshed Taycan has a bigger standard battery and more theater through simulated gears.

title: Uber, Stellantis, And Wayve Want To Blanket The World In Robotaxis
summary: Uber seems to have spent the past year attaching itself to every major robotaxi effort it can, and now Stellantis is joining that list.

title: Tesla Owners Are Using Doll Heads To Fool Full Self-Driving
summary: Is that Dwayne Johnson driving your Tesla? Nope, just his likeness being used to defeat Tesla's driver monitoring systems.

title: BMW Is Opening i3 Orders Way Ahead Of Schedule Due To Huge Demand
summary: BMW had originally planned to open the order books for the all-electric 3 Series this fall. Customers had other ideas.

title: Rivian Lays Off Hundreds Of Workers A Week After R2 Launch
summary: Rivian cut employees from its service and customer division on Tuesday, the The Wall Street Journal reported.

title: The Audi A6 Allroad Is Now A Plug-In Hybrid Antidote To Boring SUVs
summary: Thanks to a big battery pack, the new A6 Allroad wagon can deliver nearly 60 miles of electric range on the WLTP cycle.

title: Telo's Tiny Electric Truck Moves Closer To Reality With An Important New Partner
summary: Telo's adorable truck just hit a major milestone as the company finds a manufacturing partner for the EV's body structure.

title: Full Tesla Cybercab Specs: EPA Documents Confirm Battery Size, Weight, Power, And Preliminary Range
summary: The first front-wheel-drive Tesla will weigh way less than a Model 3 but still offer plenty of range.

title: A Chinese Car Brand Says It Hired A Top Ferrari Designer. Ferrari Wants A Name
summary: Luxeed says it hired a former Ferrari chief designer. Ferrari’s China PR boss publicly challenged the claim by asking who that was.
